roadrunner 0.0.0.9028
  • Reference
  • Changelog

ares — Architecture

Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

Package layout

ares/
├── DESCRIPTION
├── NAMESPACE
├── LICENSE / LICENSE.md
├── README.md / NEWS.md
├── ARCHITECTURE.md         (this file)
├── R/
│   ├── ares-package.R      (package-level docs, useDynLib, importFrom)
│   ├── ares.R              (`ares()` generic, formula method, default method)
│   ├── predict.R           (`predict.ares()`)
│   ├── print.R             (print.ares, summary.ares, print.summary.ares, plot.ares)
│   ├── zzz.R               (.onLoad hook — currently a no-op)
│   └── RcppExports.R       (auto-generated by Rcpp::compileAttributes)
├── src/
│   ├── ares.cpp            (~1660 lines — MARS engine: forward fast-LS,
│   │                        Givens-downdate backward, AVX2 q-loops,
│   │                        fast-MARS priority cache, incremental QR)
│   ├── Makevars            (RcppParallel + OpenMP linker flags; no BLAS)
│   ├── Makevars.win        (Windows variant)
│   └── RcppExports.cpp     (auto-generated)
├── tests/
│   ├── testthat.R
│   └── testthat/
│       ├── test-autotune.R     (autotune grid + halving + warmstart)
│       ├── test-bagging.R      (n.boot composes with autotune)
│       ├── test-basic.R
│       ├── test-cv-pruning.R   (pmethod="cv", nfold, ncross, 1-SE)
│       ├── test-edge-cases.R
│       ├── test-fit-friedman.R (R^2 on DGP signal)
│       ├── test-fit-mtcars.R
│       ├── test-print.R
│       ├── test-speed.R
│       └── test-threading.R
├── vignettes/
│   └── ares.Rmd
├── inst/
│   └── sims/               (Monte Carlo benchmark; not exercised by R CMD check)
│       ├── dgp_friedman.R
│       ├── dgp_additive.R
│       ├── dgp_interaction.R
│       ├── run_mc.R        (smoke grid orchestrator)
│       ├── analyse.R
│       └── results/        (output CSVs)
└── man/                    (auto-generated .Rd files)

Algorithm — gaussian-only core MARS

Forward pass (mars_fit_cpp in src/ares.cpp)

  1. Initialize basis B with intercept column. Fit OLS, compute residual r = y − fitted.
  2. Per forward step:
    1. Build orthonormal basis Q of current B via modified Gram–Schmidt (build_Q helper, in-place, returns rank Mq).
    2. Enumerate (parent, variable) pairs respecting interaction-degree cap.
    3. Dispatch RcppParallel::parallelFor(0, npairs, worker). Each worker scans eligible knots for its assigned (parent, var) slice and computes the joint RSS reduction of the hinge pair via Gram–Schmidt residual projection against Q.
    4. Reduce: pick the (parent, var, knot) with maximum reduction. Tie-break by smaller variable, then smaller knot, then smaller parent.
    5. Add the hinge pair (s = +1, then s = −1) as new basis columns.
    6. Refit OLS via in-place Householder QR (hand-rolled in ols_qr); update RSS and residual.
    7. If relative RSS improvement < thresh, stop.

Backward pass

  • Sequential subset selection. At each step, drop the term whose removal causes the smallest RSS increase (subject to keeping the intercept).
  • Compute GCV at each subset size: RSS / (n * (1 - (M + penalty*(M-1)/2)/n)^2).
  • Select the subset minimizing GCV.
  • Refit OLS on selected basis to obtain final coefficients.

Parallelism (ForwardWorker in src/ares.cpp)

  • Uses RcppParallel TBB (parallelFor).
  • Worker signature takes raw const double* pointers — no Rcpp::* API calls.
  • Each worker writes only to its own slot in out (no false sharing).
  • When nthreads <= 1, the dispatch site short-circuits to a serial loop, bypassing TBB scheduler overhead.

Numerical strategy

  • All arithmetic in double.
  • QR is hand-rolled (Householder) — no LAPACK dependency, eliminates BLAS/LAPACK linker complexity.
  • Rank guards: candidate columns whose perpendicular norm is below 1e3 * eps * scale are skipped.
  • Residual is computed from full OLS each step (not residual-update tricks); this trades speed for numerical robustness in v0.0.0.9000.

R surface

  • ares() — generic with formula and default methods.
  • predict.ares() — accepts data.frame or matrix; uses mars_basis_cpp to build the basis on newdata, then multiplies by coefficients.
  • print.ares(), summary.ares(), print.summary.ares(), plot.ares() — standard S3 methods.

Thread-safety contract

  • The C++ entry mars_fit_cpp calls RcppParallel::parallelFor only after flattening Rcpp objects to std::vector<double> and std::vector<int>.
  • Workers read those flattened buffers via const double* / const int* pointers and write only to a per-pair output slot.
  • RcppParallel::setThreadOptions(numThreads = ...) is called from R-side ares.default() before entering C++; the C++ code observes the count via the nthreads parameter to choose serial vs parallel paths.

Performance characteristics (v0.0.0.9001)

  • Forward pass dominates runtime.
  • Per-step cost: O(n × M²) for Q construction + O(npairs × (n + K) × M_q) for knot scoring (Friedman fast-LS prefix sums), where K = #eligible knots per pair (typically ~50), M_q = rank of Q (typically ≤ M).
  • For Friedman-1 n=1500 deg=2: ares-1t ~1.1s (was ~22s in v0.0.0.9000), earth ~0.05s. Median single-thread ratio vs earth across the inst/sims grid: ~28× (was ~152×).
  • The remaining wall-clock gap is now dominated by serial parts (build_Q, ols_qr) rather than the per-knot inner loop. Parallel scaling has shrunk for the same reason and is a v0.2 roadmap item.

Design decisions worth knowing

  • No BLAS/LAPACK linkage: the hand-rolled QR is sufficient at the M ≤ 200 basis-term scale typical of MARS. This also dodges system-config issues (the dev machine had a broken MKL config that wouldn’t link).
  • Selected.terms is 1-indexed: returned to R as 1-based indices for consistency with earth’s convention.
  • Term labels: (Intercept), h(x-cut), h(cut-x), products joined by * for interactions — matches earth’s labeling.
  • Drop constant columns: emit a single warning(), drop, continue.
  • No global state: .onLoad is a no-op. Thread count is set per-call via setThreadOptions. No package-level options.

Testing strategy

  • tests/testthat/ — 17 test_that blocks, 38 expectations.
  • Categories: structure, predict identity, formula method, edge cases (constant column, single predictor, degree=2 monotonicity, input validation), parity vs earth (Friedman-1 + mtcars), threading determinism, print/summary smoke, speed scaling.
  • inst/sims/run_mc.R — separate Monte Carlo grid (24 cells × 5 reps) for numerical-parity benchmarking. Not exercised by R CMD check.

CRAN compliance

  • DESCRIPTION software names quoted: ‘Rcpp’, ‘RcppParallel’, ‘earth’.
  • Title in Title Case.
  • Authors@R format.
  • No T/F, no global set.seed(), no bare print()/cat() outside print methods, no installed.packages(), no <<-, no .GlobalEnv writes.
  • Examples and vignettes capped at 2 threads.
  • inst/sims/ is not auto-exercised.

What is NOT implemented (deliberate scope)

  • glm responses, multi-response y, observation weights.
  • pmethod ∈ {"exhaustive", "forward", "seqrep", "cv"}.
  • fast.k / fast.beta heuristics.
  • ANOVA decomposition / evimp variable importance.
  • linpreds / Auto.linpreds (linear-only directions are reserved as dirs == 2 but not currently produced by the forward pass).

On this page
